perturbation.original_text: If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy, or the receipt of a waiver by TBI, EnCana or any of its Representatives should nonetheless, based on the advice of such party's counsel, disclose the Evaluation Material and/or the facts or information covered by Section 2, EnCana or its Representative may, without liability hereunder, disclose only that portion of the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or information that such counsel advises is legally required to be disclosed; provided that EnCana gives TBI written notice of the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or other information to be disclosed as far in advance of its disclosure as is reasonably practicable and exercises its reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or other information, including, without limitation, by cooperating with TBI to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or information.
perturbation.changed_text: If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy, or the receipt of a waiver by TBI, EnCana or any of its Representatives should nonetheless, based on the advice of such party's counsel, disclose the Evaluation Material and/or the facts or information covered by Section 2, EnCana or its Representative may, without liability hereunder, disclose only that portion of the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or information that such counsel deems appropriate to be disclosed; provided that EnCana gives TBI written notice of the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or other information to be disclosed as far in advance of its disclosure as is reasonably practicable and exercises its reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or other information, including, without limitation, by cooperating with TBI to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or information.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, disclosure was limited to what counsel advises is 'legally required.' The modification allows disclosure of what counsel deems 'appropriate.' 'Legally required' is objective, while 'appropriate' is subjective and broader. This creates a contradiction in the standard for permissible disclosure. Now the definition of the Evaluation Material can have two different standards that might be contradictory. This also introduces a contradiction within Section 3, where the 'portion of Evaluation Material' is being handled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy, or the receipt of a waiver by TBI, EnCana or any of its Representatives should nonetheless, based on the advice of such party's counsel, disclose the Evaluation Material and/or the facts or information covered by Section 2, EnCana or its Representative may, without liability hereunder, disclose only that portion of the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or information that such counsel deems appropriate to be disclosed; provided that EnCana gives TBI written notice of the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or other information to be disclosed as far in advance of its disclosure as is reasonably practicable and exercises its reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or other information, including, without limitation, by cooperating with TBI to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or information.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits disclosure to what counsel advises is 'legally required,' which is an objective standard. The changed text broadens this to what counsel 'deems appropriate,' a subjective standard. The explanation states that this difference creates a contradiction in the standard for permissible disclosure, introducing ambiguity in how the 'Evaluation Material' is handled within Section 3. The Contradicted Text (which is identical to the Changed Text) suffers from this ambiguity. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the termination of any discussions or the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their obligations of confidentiality hereunder for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the termination of any discussions or the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their obligations of confidentiality hereunder for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof, unless otherwise agreed in writing by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states an explicit 18-month confidentiality period. The addition of 'unless otherwise agreed in writing' introduces the possibility of altering this period. This creates a contradiction, as the confidentiality obligations are no longer fixed. This impacts Section 9(c) regarding the term termination of obligations under the contract, where confidentiality can be terminated at will now, rather than have it being terminated by the eighteen months of original agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary other than paragraph 7 above, all of the obligations of the parties hereunder shall (if not sooner terminated) terminate on the second anniversary of the date hereof; provided, however, that no such termination shall relieve a party from liability for any breach by such party of the terms hereof that exists on the date of such termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'unless otherwise agreed in writing by both parties' to the confidentiality clause. This directly contradicts section (c) because it allows for the confidentiality obligations to be terminated at any time by written agreement, while the original clause in section (c) specifies termination after the second anniversary. This creates a strong contradiction as the duration and method of termination of confidentiality are no longer aligned, allowing it to be terminated at will now, impacting Section 9(c) regarding the term termination of obligations under the contract.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of 18 months from the date hereof, EnCana agrees that it and its affiliates shall not, without the prior written request of TBI, directly or indirectly, alone or in concert with others, (a) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, by purchase, or otherwise, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of more than 1% of any class of voting securities issued by TBI or any material assets of TBI or any securities or material assets of any subsidiary of TBI, (b) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (c) make, or in any way participate in, any "solicitation" of "proxies" (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the United States Securities and Exchange Commission) with respect to the securities of TBI, or advise or seek to influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, or giving of consents with respect to, any securities of TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (d) seek or propose to influence or control (whether though a "group," as such term is used in Rule 13d-5 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 or otherwise) the management, board of directors, policies or affairs of TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (e) to the extent doing so would require the public disclosure of such action by TBI, make any request to waive or amend any provision of this Section 5, (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with any of the foregoing or (g) encourage any third party to do any of the foregoing; provided, however, that this Section 5 shall terminate with respect to EnCana if a person or entity (other than EnCana or an affiliate of EnCana) acquires, enters an agreement to acquire, or publicly proposes to acquire, directly or indirectly, by merger or otherwise, more than 50% of the voting securities of TBI (i.e., those securities of TBI that are entitled to participate in the annual election of directors), or otherwise acquires, enters an agreement to acquire, or publicly proposes to acquire, the ability to control the management or policies of TBI.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of 18 months from the date hereof, EnCana agrees that it and its affiliates shall not, without the prior written consent of TBI, directly or indirectly, alone or in concert with others, (a) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, by purchase, or otherwise, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of more than 1% of any class of voting securities issued by TBI or any material assets of TBI or any securities or material assets of any subsidiary of TBI, (b) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (c) make, or in any way participate in, any "solicitation" of "proxies" (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the United States Securities and Exchange Commission) with respect to the securities of TBI, or advise or seek to influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, or giving of consents with respect to, any securities of TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (d) seek or propose to influence or control (whether though a "group," as such term is used in Rule 13d-5 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 or otherwise) the management, board of directors, policies or affairs of TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (e) to the extent doing so would require the public disclosure of such action by TBI, make any request to waive or amend any provision of this Section 5, (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with any of the foregoing or (g) encourage any third party to do any of the foregoing; provided, however, that this Section 5 shall terminate with respect to EnCana if a person or entity (other than EnCana or an affiliate of EnCana) acquires, enters an agreement to acquire, or publicly proposes to acquire, directly or indirectly, by merger or otherwise, more than 49% of the voting securities of TBI (i.e., those securities of TBI that are entitled to participate in the annual election of directors), or otherwise acquires, enters an agreement to acquire, or publicly proposes to acquire, the ability to control the management or policies of TBI.
perturbation.explanation: The original trigger for the termination of the Standstill Agreement in Section 5 is the acquisition of 'more than 50%' of the voting securities of TBI by a third party. The modified version changes this to 'more than 49%'. This creates a contradiction, because a party acquiring exactly 50% of the voting securities now triggers termination under the original definition, but NOT under the modified one, and vice versa, acquisition of 49.5% securities would have NOT triggered, but does now.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of 18 months from the date hereof, EnCana agrees that it and its affiliates shall not, without the prior written request of TBI, directly or indirectly, alone or in concert with others, (a) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, by purchase, or otherwise, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of more than 1% of any class of voting securities issued by TBI or any material assets of TBI or any securities or material assets of any subsidiary of TBI, (b) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (c) make, or in any way participate in, any "solicitation" of "proxies" (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the United States Securities and Exchange Commission) with respect to the securities of TBI, or advise or seek to influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, or giving of consents with respect to, any securities of TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (d) seek or propose to influence or control (whether though a "group," as such term is used in Rule 13d-5 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 or otherwise) the management, board of directors, policies or affairs of TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (e) to the extent doing so would require the public disclosure of such action by TBI, make any request to waive or amend any provision of this Section 5, (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with any of the foregoing or (g) encourage any third party to do any of the foregoing; provided, however, that this Section 5 shall terminate with respect to EnCana if a person or entity (other than EnCana or an affiliate of EnCana) acquires, enters an agreement to acquire, or publicly proposes to acquire, directly or indirectly, by merger or otherwise, more than 49% of the voting securities of TBI (i.e., those securities of TBI that are entitled to participate in the annual election of directors), or otherwise acquires, enters an agreement to acquire, or publicly proposes to acquire, the ability to control the management or policies of TBI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the threshold for termination of the standstill agreement from 'more than 50%' to 'more than 49%'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which now operates under a different trigger threshold. For example, if a third party acquires exactly 50% of the voting securities, the original text would trigger termination, but the changed text, as reflected in the contradicted text, would not. This creates a strong and clear inconsistency within the document regarding the conditions under which the standstill agreement terminates. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
